mechanism_narrative: Robust and increasing retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, fostering positive sentiment for equity markets and driving S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat on average during periods of sustained positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) are elevated, it signals a restrictive monetary policy environment. This increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings' present value, negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive or flat when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) often signifies a late-cycle economic expansion. While initially positive, it can lead to wage inflation and central bank tightening, eventually becoming a headwind for corporate profits and equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive on average during periods when UNRATE is consistently below 5% for more than six months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening consumer and business confidence, signaling a broader economic slowdown that will negatively impact corporate earnings and subsequently S&P 500 equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive or flat following a month-over-month decline in HOUST exceeding 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, which is a key driver of overall economic growth, supporting corporate profitability and leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat despite consistent positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) are growing, but the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is also rising, it suggests underlying structural issues or weakening labor demand despite headline job creation, which is a negative signal for future economic growth and equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive when PAYEMS is growing but UNRATE is simultaneously rising.

mechanism_narrative: A high and consistently rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This increases the cost of capital, dampens economic activity, and leads to lower equity valuations over a longer investment horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND has increased for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and has been rising for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and sustained GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong and expanding economy, which provides a favorable macro environment for corporate earnings and, consequently, drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained positive GDPC1 growth.
